transcript.text: Are we going to invite Lawrence to these?
I think Andy mentioned yesterday to pause on that.
Okay.
Thank you.
Thank you.
I'm muted.
Sorry.
Nope.
Sorry, I'm late.
We're just wrapping up another call.
Andrew and Elaine will be here in a sec.
Hi.
How's everyone doing today?
you.
Good, how are you?
Doing well, doing well.
We can probably just get started and I'll jump in.
Okay, okay, cool.
All right, let me just share my screen.
Okay, so I have a couple of updates.
So Eric did meet with the brand team yesterday.
So Eric, if you want to just do a quick update of what you talked about so the team has visibility.
Yeah, so we were just going through.
Yeah.
Featured images, so trying to get some stuff from the team on just design guidelines and any design assets that we can get our hands on so we can form our team.
We did do some featured image work to try to get as close as we can to kind of what you guys currently have, but with the additional assets, we'll get even closer.
We also discussed the campaign calendar that they have for next year for any of the kind of longer form gated assets that we want to go after.
Those will most likely be something we cover kind of once we get out of Strategy Sprint, just given the time it takes to develop those assets from a creative standpoint.
But we at least will be aligned with the campaigns that are planned for Q1, Q2, and Q3 next year.
Other than that, it was really just kind of a meet and greet with the team to just better understand their process, who's in charge of what.
So now we...
To include Pete for anything that will need to be published to the blog.
You can actually just run it by Elaine.
We were just talking to Pete about this process.
I think he's got a lot on his plate.
We'll let Elaine project management, project manage it and coordinate with the people internally who need to improve it and all that stuff.
So, Andrew, Elaine, that worked for you all rather than getting Pete involved on this side of things and just added another person to it.
Yeah, as long as we get respective buy-in from PMM, Creative, and such, and they are good with the output, then, yeah, I think that we're in good shape.
Yeah, I think based on what we just talked about with Pete, you know, us reviewing it, putting the final touches on it with PMM, with Creative.
If he wants to be involved, we can get him involved, but it doesn't sound like he needs or wants to be super involved with all the pieces.
So, happy to kind of keep him out of this as much as we can for now.
Yep.
Elaine, you good with that?
Yeah.
Cool.
Okay, great.
We did finalize the calibration.
So, according to Elaine's comments last week, we made the tone like slightly more conversational.
And I was wondering, Elaine and, you know, everybody else, if you had additional feedback or if it was just good to publish.
Yeah, I would say the, we're talking about the final calibration draft, right?
Yeah.
one that was great.
This one, yeah, you're in the talk, yeah.
I mean, I would say the tone is better than the first one.
Like, the first line alone is so much different from the first draft.
I would say the tone is better.
But I can't really sign off on all of the technical aspects of the draft.
So I think we'll have to wait for the EMMs to give their feedback on the technical details on that.
Did any of them get back to you, Elaine, on that?
Are we still waiting for them?
No one answered, gave feedback yet.
So I'll try to get in touch with all of That's fine.
I'll reply to your email that you sent them this afternoon and try to push a little harder.
Thanks.
Yep.
Okay, great.

Yeah, mean, as soon as, I mean, you have, since you'll be taking care of the publishing, I think what we can, what I can do is, it's done reviewing basically like from Elaine's and from like a tone perspective.
So I'm just going to change this to, um, um,
Actually, let me add a new status.
I'll just add like a PMM review status so you know.
Can I also, I wonder if you sent this also to Pete or, because I feel like he would also have, you know, insights about the language and the tone, right?
Because I remember he mentioned earlier in our call about, you know, having too much M-dashes.
So I wonder if this has been sent out to Pete Reuter and maybe I could send this calibration blog and get this feedback as well.
Sure.
Yeah, I think you should, Elaine.
I think, you know, any internal experts that we have that can sign off or give feedback on the calibration product is going to help reduce overhead needed in the future.
Like we can feel confident about the pieces coming in.
End.
End.
Yeah, less time reviewing each piece.
Yeah, was just about to say, do we, can we please drop the link for the final calibration?

I don't think I've seen this one yet, and I was looking at my email, and it's very possible that I missed it, but let's just make sure that this is the document that's sent around for everyone to review again, because I think the email was...
Yeah, was the first draft.
Yeah, right.
Exactly.
Yep.
Perfect, thank you for that, Aida.
No worries.
Okay, yeah, I think, you know, feel free to take your time, like, I'm going to be, we're going to be waiting for the feedback.
In the meantime, we are working on five blogs this week.
You can find them here, but we also changed the status, so they're right now in production.
And we do want to deliver them by the end of the week.
So, yeah, any feedback that's provided on the calibration will also be taken into consideration appearance if to more information.
For, like, next week's content and the feedback for the existing ones.
And then for the featured images, so Erik did meet with the brand team.
They sent over some assets for just, like, the visual guidelines.
But before that, Katya, who's our designer, she did put together, like, a pipeline just to see if we could generate images that are kind of similar.
to the one you already have on your blog.
And I think you did mention, like, okay, there's a lot of humans in the featured images.
But this is what they look like right now.
So we can check, like, we can fix the aspect ratio to make it, like, more rectangular.
But, yeah, like, this is AI, I think this, yeah, this is AI generated, like, the human.
Yeah, not bad.
I mean, I, not.
Not a creative resource to be able to fully sign off on things and seems like the AI favored pointing towards something in a lot of these.

But yeah, as long as we get buy-in from creative directors and such, I feel confident with how this looks personally, but I don't think any of us are unfortunately in a place to say like, yeah, let's go for it kind of thing.
Yeah, sure.
Now, I think we can kind of go back and fold in the resources that the brand team shared with us yesterday.
So we can like come up with different versions, maybe some that don't include humans.
And then we can kind of share those as well and see what you think.
Great.
Okay, perfect.
And then for just generally, like, I think...
I don't know, I want to make, like, the content review process as kind of as smooth as possible.
So my idea was we could set up an automation in Airtable to send an email to a group of people when a draft is ready for review.
So let us know, like, Elaine, do you want to be on that email or should we also include other people?
I think when a draft is ready, it's probably the three of us here.
Okay.
And then we'll just include all the internal coordination.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Okay, and then when it's ready to publish, who should we, should we also tag, the three of you?
Yeah.
Okay, okay, sounds good.
Perfect, so I'll set that up today.
I'll that
Okay.
Okay.
And then I did send you an email last week around the design resources for the gated assets.
So Andy did confirm that our designers can do that work.
It'll take them around like two to three weeks to put the PDF together, but it is something that we can do in-house once we kind of like create the gated asset.

So, yeah, I think I was thinking, you know, this is something that we can start working on in phase two, but I could take some of the suggestions that I made a couple of weeks ago and like further flesh them out and like maybe provide like a brief outline, you know, to see like what it would actually look like and then share it out with you.
So you have like better idea of the structure and, you know, any additions that you want to make.
But like I said, the main idea was to take the bigger report that you had on like small business.
Like cyber security, cyber threats, and then like pull out more specific topics and turn them into gated assets.
So I think maybe an outline would be more helpful to decide, okay, like this is what we want to focus on first.
Yeah, I think so.
Personally, I think that'd be very helpful for our creative team to see.
They may, you know, there may be more questions or internal time necessary for something more broad, like a gated asset versus a feature image of a blog.
So we'll definitely want any, you know, visual representation to be provided that we can show them.
Okay.
And I may have missed this, but is this included in our, in our contract or is there another charge associated with that?
So the design, the, the design portion is.
It's included, like the actual design work, yeah.
Okay, great.
And I think we, I mean, yeah, for the articles, we will do them as well.
So the blog content is also going to be produced alongside.
I just need to talk to Andy to see how we can balance these two when we're working on, because the gated assets, they're not going to be too long.
I think they're probably going to be as long as a blog post.
So, yeah, I can talk to Andy and see how we can, like, scope this exactly in the contract.
That's great.
I was just about to say, like, if they're about the same length of a blog, we can expect that each gated asset would be, like, one deliverable in that total bucket.
Okay, that makes sense.
Yeah.
Okay.
All right, cool.
I think those are all the updates that I had.
Did you guys have any other questions or anything else you want to talk about?
So, I guess just to confirm, the content calendar that we
Right now, these are all blog posts, right?
So we're going to get a separate set of topics for gated assets.
Yes.
So I think when you go to our notion, if you go to docs, you can see ideas for gated resources.
And this is where I've only provided like two so far.
And I'm going to add more.
And then I'm going to like create outlines next week that I'm going to show you so you can kind of, you know, see more beyond like just like the rough idea.
So, yeah, these are these are the ideas that I had.
it was mostly pulling topics from the annual threat report that we can kind of package as like an individual gated asset.
Thank you.
Yeah.
But the content calendar is just, it's just.
So yeah, let us know.
I mean, I know that you approved additional topics last week, but if you have any suggestions, like I know that you've gone through the calendar a couple of times now.
If you have any suggestions around topics that you feel like we have missed or that, you know, we should generate, just let me know.
I'm happy to do additional keyword research and like provide more assignments here.
Yeah, for sure.
Okay, great.
I think if that's it, I can give you 10 minutes back.
But yeah, I'm available over email if you need anything else.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
See you next week.
Bye-bye.
Thanks.
Bye, all.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content progress, review processes, and future asset strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=222.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Review Workflow:** Elaine now owns blog PM, replacing Pete to streamline approvals. An Airtable automation will notify Nicholas, Andrew, and Elaine when drafts are ready.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=299.0)
  - [**Calibration Blog Blocked:** The revised draft awaits technical review from EMMs. Nicholas will escalate to unblock it, as Elaine approved the tone but cannot validate the technical content.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
  - [**Gated Asset Strategy:** GrowthX will create outlines for gated assets (e.g., PDFs from the cybersecurity report) for N-able's creative team. Each asset will count as one blog deliverable.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
  - [**Featured Image Direction:** GrowthX will refine AI-generated images using N-able brand assets, exploring non-human options to align with creative director feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)

## Topics

### Content Review & Publishing Workflow

  - [**Problem:** The previous review process was inefficient, with Pete as a bottleneck.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=299.0)
  - [**Solution:** Elaine now owns blog project management, coordinating all internal approvals (PMM, Creative, etc.).](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=299.0)
  - [**Automation:** An Airtable automation will notify Nicholas, Andrew, and Elaine when drafts are ready for review and again when they are ready for publication.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)

### Calibration Blog Review

  - [**Status:** The revised draft has a more conversational tone, approved by Elaine.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
  - [**Blocker:** Publication is blocked pending technical review from EMMs. Nicholas will escalate to get their feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
  - [**Action:** Aida will share the correct, final draft link, as the initial email contained an old version. Elaine will also send it to Pete Reuter for a language/tone check.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=494.0)

### Featured Image Development

  - [**Progress:** GrowthX's designer, Katya, created initial AI-generated images to match the current blog style.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)
  - [**Feedback:** Andrew noted the images are "not bad" but require creative director approval.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)
  - [**Action:** GrowthX will refine the images using N-able brand assets and explore non-human options to address feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=687.0)

### Gated Asset Strategy

  - [**Goal:** Repurpose content from the annual small business cybersecurity report into smaller, topic-specific gated assets (e.g., PDFs).](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=834.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
      - [GrowthX will create detailed outlines for N-able's creative team to review.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=803.0)
      - [GrowthX designers will handle the PDF design (2–3 week turnaround).](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
  - [**Contracting:** Each gated asset will count as one blog deliverable. Aida will confirm this scope with Andy.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=888.0)

### Content Production

  - [**Current Work:** Five blog posts are in production this week for end-of-week delivery.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)
  - [**Feedback Loop:** Any feedback from the Calibration blog review will be applied to future content.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)
  - [**Topic Suggestions:** N-able can suggest new topics for GrowthX to research.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=1018.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Nicholas:** Escalate with EMMs to get technical feedback on the Calibration blog.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=421.0)
  - [**Elaine:** Send the final Calibration blog draft to Pete Reuter for a language/tone check.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=494.0)
  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=494.0)
      - [Share the correct, final Calibration blog link with the team.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=544.0)
      - [Set up the Airtable review notification automation.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
      - [Create and share detailed outlines for gated assets.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=803.0)
      - [Confirm gated asset scope (one deliverable each) with Andy.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=888.0)
  - [**GrowthX:** Refine featured images using N-able brand assets, exploring non-human options.](https://fathom.video/share/7ZJWZiUzWLA8gxqwD1uCGHpSVzXShav_?tab=summary&timestamp=687.0)

action_items.description: Reply to Elaine's EMM email re: calibration; push for technical feedback
Email Pete Reuter the final calibration draft for review
Send final calibration draft link to Nicholas, Andrew, Elaine
Produce/share featured image variants (human/no-human, correct ratio) w/ Creative; then get sign-off
Set up Airtable review automations: notify Nicholas, Andrew, Elaine on draft ready; tag on publish
Draft gated asset outlines; share w/ Andrew, Elaine, Nicholas
Align w/ Andrew on gated asset scope/contract; confirm 1 gated asset = 1 blog deliverable
